nodes.html: AI training for the people who run the work.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 12.73
Element has sufficient color contrast of 6.33
Element has sufficient color contrast of 14.36

nodes.html: Broad-audience programs designed to lift the AI capability of the people who do the work. The format is a cohort, not a webinar. Participants commit to live sessions over four to eight weeks, with faculty leading and operators contributing.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 14.36

nodes.html: Content is built for the role, not the headline: what the AI tools actually do, what they do not, and where human judgment still matters most. Cohorts run as a series of waves so leadership can absorb and apply between groups.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 6.33
Element has sufficient color contrast of 14.36

nodes.html: Half-day, one-day, and multi-day formats for senior leaders. Built for the people who decide where AI capital goes and how the workforce is asked to change.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 14.36

nodes.html: Topics include enterprise AI strategy, AI risk and governance, AI in customer operations, and AI in product development. The default format is a working session: short briefings from faculty and operators, then structured work on the participant's actual problem.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 14.36

nodes.html: Outputs are decisions and next-step commitments, not slides.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 6.33
Element has sufficient color contrast of 14.36
Element has sufficient color contrast of 14.36

nodes.html: A custom build typically adds a few weeks to the timeline depending on depth. The faculty pool draws from the same university partners, so academic integrity is consistent with everything else the institute delivers.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 6.33

nodes.html: The network is the part of the partnership that survives the program.

nodes.html: Capability uplift across the cohort, measured by program assessments and by management feedback over the months that follow.

nodes.html: Project velocity, especially on AI initiatives that were stalled because the team did not have the confidence to ship.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 14.36

nodes.html: Internal community: a group of people who now share a vocabulary, a set of references, and a working relationship that did not exist before.

nodes.html: Broad-audience programs designed to lift the AI capability of the people who do the work. The format is a cohort, not a webinar. Participants commit to live sessions over four to eight weeks, with faculty leading and operators contributing.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Content is built for the role, not the headline: what the AI tools actually do, what they do not, and where human judgment still matters most. Cohorts run as a series of waves so leadership can absorb and apply between groups.

nodes.html: Half-day, one-day, and multi-day formats for senior leaders. Built for the people who decide where AI capital goes and how the workforce is asked to change.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Topics include enterprise AI strategy, AI risk and governance, AI in customer operations, and AI in product development. The default format is a working session: short briefings from faculty and operators, then structured work on the participant's actual problem.
